content: When assets are generated, audit that each requested asset appears in a loader table, decodes into an Image, and is eventually used by drawImage rather than replaced by a procedural placeholder.
Audit generated sprites for sane alpha coverage and visual distinctness between entity classes; flag blank/opaque cards and accidental same-looking role sprites.

Load every generated image into an Image and await img.decode() (or onload) per asset, but do NOT block requestAnimationFrame on the whole batch — start RAF immediately so canvas/input stay alive, kick off loadAssets() in the background, and draw a labeled MISSING fallback for any sprite still decoding. Drawing an undecoded image on a given frame renders nothing that frame; the next frame after decode succeeds must use drawImage.

If a generated asset is still decoding or fails to load (404/decode error), draw a visible labeled procedural MISSING marker (e.g. magenta box + 'MISSING:key' text) for THAT entity only and keep the game running — never let a missing file become steady-state art (bare colored squares with no label). Once the PNG decodes, drawImage must replace the marker automatically.

Reference assets through a single ASSETS map keyed by name with the exact relative paths the generator wrote; load each into an Image and draw with drawImage. Wrong/guessed paths 404 silently and the game shows placeholders instead of art.

For multi-frame sprite animations, generate a parent key frame then derive sibling frames with from_image so pose and palette stay consistent; do not txt2img each frame independently (style drifts between frames).

When regenerating an asset mid-session, reuse the SAME asset key/filename so the existing loader picks up the new file with no code change; renaming forces a loader rewrite and orphans the old reference.

For sprite sheets, each frame's source rect (sx,sy,sw,sh) must line up with the atlas cell grid; an off-by-one origin or wrong cell size clips or garbles frames. Compute coords from a known cell size, do not eyeball them.

Generated sounds will be silent until the AudioContext is resumed inside a user gesture (browser autoplay policy). Resume it on the first pointerdown/keydown, then play; never assume audio works on load.

Draw each generated sprite at its natural aspect ratio (use the image's width/height), not stretched into an arbitrary square; squashed art is a common cause of a game looking broken even when logic is fine.

Draw in explicit layers: background first, then world entities (optionally depth-sorted), then the HUD last. Drawing the background after entities hides sprites behind a filled rectangle and looks like nothing rendered.

Never bake changing values (score, names, timers) into a generated sprite image; draw dynamic text in code with fillText so it can update. Baked text freezes at its initial value and cannot reflect game state.
